articles.title: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Available to Stream
articles.excerpt: The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 07:01. 4 source updates have been combined into this article.

The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.

Latest update

A BBC crime drama called 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is currently available to stream for free on the platform U. The series, which first aired in 2015, follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unsolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features various locations, including Lower Falinge in Rochdale and Manchester. The narrative centres around Claire, who, after leaving Manchester due to the unsettling nature of a case she could not solve, is drawn back to the city and her former partner, DCI John Hind, played by Johnny Harris. The cast also includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, and Richard Rankin.

The plot thickens as two victims are discovered at a Manchester building site, linked to the serial killer Claire had previously investigated. The series explores her efforts to piece together clues from both the past and present to confront the killer and her own demons.

In addition to filming in Rochdale and Manchester, some scenes were shot in Argyll, Scotland, which served as a stand-in for the Western Isles. The decision to film on the mainland was reportedly due to logistical considerations. Highlands and Islands Conservative MSP Jamie McGrigor previously noted that some people were disappointed by the filming location choice but expressed hope that it would boost tourism in the region.

Earlier developments

A crime drama called 'From Darkness', which was filmed in Rochdale, is now streaming for free on U. The series originally aired on the BBC in 2015 and follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unresolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features scenes filmed in Lower Falinge, Rochdale, as well as various locations in Manchester and Scotland. The narrative centres around Claire, who returns to Manchester after leaving due to the unsettling nature of a case she could not solve. She is drawn back to confront her past and her former partner, DCI John Hind, played by Johnny Harris.

In addition to Duff and Harris, the cast includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, Leanne Best, Richard Rankin, Emma Edmonson, Lyndsey Marshal, Adrian Rawlins, and Josh Taylor. The plot thickens as Claire investigates two victims found at a Manchester building site, linking them to the serial killer she once pursued.

'From Darkness' has received positive reviews on social media and IMDb, with viewers praising its engaging storyline and performances. The series consists of four one-hour episodes, making it accessible for binge-watching. It is currently available for streaming on U.

Update timeline

  • 20 July 2026, 07:01: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Available to Stream
  • 20 July 2026, 07:01: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Streaming for Free

articles.title: Police Seek Help to Locate Wanted Man from Rochdale Area
articles.excerpt: Officers are looking for Caylum Smith, who is wanted for failing to appear in court and burglary. He is believed to frequent several areas including Blackley and Salford.
articles.content_html:
Officers are appealing for the public's help to trace a wanted man from the Rochdale area. Caylum Smith, born on 6 August 1997, is wanted for failure to appear at court and burglary.

Smith is believed to frequent the Charleston, Blackley, and Salford areas. Police have urged anyone who may have seen him or has information regarding his whereabouts to come forward.

The appeal comes as part of ongoing efforts to locate individuals wanted by the police in connection with various offences. Authorities are keen to resolve this matter swiftly.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Edenfield Road to Close for Roadworks from 15 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Edenfield Road in Rochdale will be temporarily closed for roadworks starting on 15 August 2026, affecting traffic flow in the area.
articles.content_html:
Part of Edenfield Road will be temporarily closed from 15 August 2026. The closure will begin 10 metres west of the junction with Overtown Lane and extend 674 metres in an easterly direction.

The roadworks are expected to impact traffic flow on Edenfield Road and surrounding routes, including Spotland Road, Sandy Lane, and Bury Road.

Drivers may need to seek alternative routes during this period. Further details about the roadworks and affected areas can be found on the Rochdale Borough Council website.

Residents and commuters are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the road closure may lead to delays.

articles.title: Rochdale Councillors Approve Pay Rise Despite Cost of Living Concerns
articles.excerpt: Rochdale councillors have voted to increase their annual allowances despite objections related to the ongoing cost of living crisis affecting local families.
articles.content_html:
A vote at Rochdale Town Hall has resulted in an increase in the annual allowances for councillors. The current allowance of £12,996.09 will rise to £13,515 following the decision made on July 16.

The pay rise has sparked significant debate among councillors, with members of the Reform and Workers Party voicing strong opposition. Councillor Mohammed Shafiq from the Workers Party described the increase as 'inappropriate' given the financial struggles faced by many families in the borough. He stated that it is not the right time to approve such an increase when so many are struggling to make ends meet.

In response, Labour councillors defended the rise, arguing that the new allowance remains lower than those in other Greater Manchester councils. Councillor Kath Bromfield stated that the increase would help make politics more accessible to those without substantial financial means, while Councillor Dylan Williams noted that the remuneration equates to less than minimum wage considering the time commitment required for councillors.

Council leader Neil Emmott highlighted that those opposed to the increase could choose not to claim the full amount. The vote was passed by the controlling Labour group, with only the basic allowance being adjusted; all other special allowances will remain unchanged. The special allowances include payments for the leader of the council, deputy leaders, cabinet members, and committee chairs, among others.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: University of Manchester Student Researches Garden Management in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A master's student at the University of Manchester is conducting research on garden management preferences in Rochdale, focusing on biodiversity targets.
articles.content_html:
A master's student at the University of Manchester is currently completing her dissertation on garden management preferences in Rochdale, UK. The research aims to understand how these preferences impact local and national biodiversity targets.

The student, called Natalie, is exploring various aspects of garden management and its implications for the environment. Her work seeks to contribute to the broader conversation about biodiversity and sustainable practices in urban areas.

Natalie's research is particularly relevant to Rochdale Borough residents, as it addresses local gardening habits and their potential effects on biodiversity. The findings may inform future initiatives aimed at enhancing ecological awareness and practices within the community.

The study highlights the importance of individual choices in gardening and their collective impact on the environment, aligning with national efforts to meet biodiversity goals.

articles.title: Middleton McDonald's Reopens Following Major Redesign
articles.excerpt: McDonald's in Middleton has reopened after a significant refurbishment aimed at enhancing customer and staff experiences, featuring updated technology and improved facilities.
articles.content_html:
A McDonald's restaurant in Middleton has reopened following a major refurbishment designed to enhance the experience for both customers and staff. The redesign includes a new layout, updated technology, and separate facilities for delivery couriers.

The restaurant, owned by local franchisee Gerald Thompson, has undergone changes as part of McDonald's latest reimagining programme. This initiative aims to improve speed, efficiency, and choice for customers. The refurbishment features a redesigned kitchen and dining area that supports digital ordering through the McDonald's app, allowing customers to order in advance and either collect their food or have it delivered to their table upon arrival.

The front counter has been restructured to create dedicated areas for different ordering methods, which McDonald's states will reduce congestion and shorten queues around self-service ordering screens. Additionally, improvements have been made behind the scenes, including a larger order assembly area and dedicated preparation spaces for dine-in meals.

Gerald Thompson, who operates 18 McDonald's restaurants across Greater Manchester, expressed pride in reinvesting in the Middleton location, which he first opened as a franchisee in 2000. He noted that the redesign provides a fresh look and enhances the experience for both customers and the restaurant's team of over eighty staff members.

articles.title: Andys Man Club Attends Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show
articles.excerpt: Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 00:07. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.

Latest update

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show as guests of the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event provided an opportunity for the group to engage with the community and promote their initiatives.

During the show, they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana and his wife, who expressed their support for the work of Andys Man Club. The club focuses on mental health awareness and support for men in the community.

The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts and families alike. It served as a platform for local organisations to connect with residents and share information about their activities.

Andys Man Club Rochdale continues to encourage open discussions about mental health and aims to reduce stigma surrounding these issues. The support from local leaders like Mayor Rana is seen as vital for their ongoing efforts.

Earlier developments

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, hosted by the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts from the local area.

During the show, members met the newly appointed Mayor of Rochdale, Faisal Rana, and his wife. The Mayor expressed support for the initiatives of Andys Man Club, which focuses on men's mental health and wellbeing.

The presence of Andys Man Club at the event highlights the group's commitment to raising awareness about mental health issues among men in the community. Their participation in local events fosters connections and encourages open discussions.

The Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show provided an opportunity for local organisations to engage with the community and promote their causes. Events like this are important for building community spirit and support.

articles.title: Rochdale Council to Tighten Controls on Houses of Multiple Occupation
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Council is set to introduce tighter controls on houses of multiple occupation (HMOs) following recent protests in Heywood organised by Jamie Thomason
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Council is set to crack down on houses of multiple occupation (HMOs) in the borough. Reform councillors have called on town hall officials to implement stricter regulations. This decision follows protests against HMOs that took place over the weekend in Heywood.

Councillor Paul O’Neill stated that the council aims to introduce an Article 4 Direction to alleviate tensions surrounding HMOs. The issue was raised by Reform councillors during the latest town hall meeting. Article 4 directives would require all HMOs, regardless of size, to obtain specific planning permission. Currently, only HMOs accommodating seven or more people require such permission.

Councillor Lee Wolf from Reform expressed that while HMO licensing is important, it does not address the location of these properties or prevent excessive clustering. He noted that residents desire a greater say in the matter and believe that HMOs are impacting local services, particularly in specific areas.

The controlling Labour group indicated that it is already planning changes. Housing boss Councillor Danny Meredith thanked Reform for their support of the Labour plan. Fellow Labour councillor Richard Jackson added that they are actively working on the issue but must follow proper processes. He emphasised that implementing Article 4 requires substantial evidence and cannot be enacted immediately. The proposal is currently undergoing local authority processes and is expected to be presented to the full council for a formal vote later this year.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.content_html:
Pakistan has issued a statement blaming Britain for the crimes committed by a rapist linked to Rochdale. The comments come amid ongoing discussions about the responsibilities of authorities in addressing such serious offences.

The statement from Pakistani officials highlights concerns regarding the handling of the case and the implications for international relations. It suggests that Britain should take greater accountability for the actions of individuals who commit crimes abroad.

This development has sparked debate about the effectiveness of cross-border cooperation in tackling crime and ensuring justice for victims.

As the situation unfolds, it remains to be seen how this will affect diplomatic relations between the two nations.

articles.title: Traffic Updates and Local News from Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Local MPs for Rochdale and Bury North have expressed their support for Prime Minister Andy Burnham's inaugural speech, which outlined plans to address rough sleeping and alleviate cost of living pressures. The Prime Minister also presented a long-term programme for government. Hathershaw College hosted Jim McMahon, MP for Oldham West, Chadderton and Royton…
articles.content_html:
Local MPs for Rochdale and Bury North have expressed their support for Prime Minister Andy Burnham's inaugural speech, which outlined plans to address rough sleeping and alleviate cost of living pressures. The Prime Minister also presented a long-term programme for government.

Hathershaw College hosted Jim McMahon, MP for Oldham West, Chadderton and Royton, to discuss its plans for a new school building and its vision for the future. Meanwhile, Reform UK's candidate for Greater Manchester Mayor has pledged support for a campaign aimed at reopening Bury Castle Armoury.

In community news, Rock Choir has launched a new group in Rochdale, offering local residents the opportunity to join the contemporary singing organisation without the need for auditions or music reading skills.

Additionally, a Bury Council environmental protection officer has been recognised in the ENDS Report Power List 2026 for her contributions to contaminated land management. Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency participated in a two-day programme with First Choice Homes Oldham as part of MP Elsie Blundell's Fly High Summer School.

articles.title: Croft Shifa Pharmacy in Rochdale Sold to First-Time Buyer
articles.excerpt: Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to first-time buyer Hossiendad Hossieni, marking a significant change in ownership for the local health centre pharmacy.
articles.content_html:
Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to a first-time buyer following a deal completed by specialist advisers. The pharmacy dispenses an average of 6,400 items each month and operates as a standard-hours health centre pharmacy.

The new owner, Hossiendad Hossieni of NHSR Limited, recognised the potential of the pharmacy, which has been run under full management for some time. Tom Young, senior business agent at Christie & Co, who handled the sale, expressed confidence in Hossieni's ability to grow the business further.

Young noted that Greater Manchester remains a hub for pharmacy transactions, with high demand from various buyer types. He stated that Croft Shifa Pharmacy presented a great opportunity for a first-time buyer and operator to enter a pharmacy that has been well-managed.

The sale is expected to enhance the pharmacy's integration with the health centre, as Hossieni aims to improve the capture rate from the facility. Young wished Hossieni the best for the future, stating he has no doubt Hossieni will take the business to the next level.

articles.title: Rochdale Pub Named Among Best in the UK by The Telegraph
articles.excerpt: The Baum in Rochdale has been included in The Telegraph's annual list of the best pubs in the UK for 2026, recognised for its food and welcoming atmosphere.
articles.content_html:
A pub in Rochdale has been named among the best in the UK. The Baum, located on Toad Lane, features in The Telegraph's annual list of The Best Pubs in the UK for 2026, which includes 500 pubs from England, 75 from Scotland, 50 from Wales, and 25 from Northern Ireland.

The selection process involved a panel of judges who assessed each pub based on criteria such as character, staff friendliness, and historical significance. Nineteen pubs from Greater Manchester made the list, with The Baum being highlighted for its food offerings, including traditional Bury black pudding served in a scotch egg and on the breakfast menu.

Although The Baum is not a historic pub, having been converted from a hardware shop in the 1980s, it has gained a reputation for its welcoming atmosphere and diverse menu. The pub offers a range of lunch and evening options, featuring classic dishes like fish and chips, as well as smaller plates such as seafood tacos and lamb kofta tacos.

The recognition from The Telegraph is likely to attract visitors from nearby areas, including Bury, who may be interested in experiencing the pub's acclaimed food and inviting environment.

articles.title: Community Payback Workers Maintain Whitworth Cemetery Grounds
articles.excerpt: A team of community payback workers has been seen mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery, demonstrating respect for the site while maintaining its appearance.
articles.content_html:
A team of community payback workers has been observed mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery. The workers were noted for their respectful approach to the headstones as they carried out their tasks.

The maintenance work was witnessed by a visitor who had come to clean and strim two graves. The visitor expressed appreciation for the efforts of the community payback team, highlighting their dedication to the upkeep of the cemetery.

Community payback schemes are designed to provide offenders with the opportunity to give back to the community through unpaid work. This initiative not only helps maintain local spaces but also fosters a sense of responsibility among participants.

Whitworth Road Cemetery serves as a significant site for the local community, and the ongoing maintenance efforts contribute to its upkeep and respect for those interred there.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption in Heywood Due to Middleton Road Changes
articles.excerpt: Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road, affecting routes throughout the town.
articles.content_html:
Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road. Reports indicate that the changes are causing significant congestion across various routes in the town.

The alterations to Middleton Road are part of an experimental scheme aimed at improving pedestrian access. However, some residents have questioned the timing of the changes, particularly as other roadworks are also taking place in the area.

Witnesses described the situation as chaotic, with delays affecting not only the immediate vicinity of the 'Hopwood Triangle' but also extending throughout Heywood. The combination of roadworks and the new pedestrianisation measures has led to frustration among drivers and pedestrians alike.

Authorities have not yet commented on the situation or provided information regarding potential adjustments to the traffic management plan.

articles.title: Councillor Speaks at Full Council Meeting in Rochdale Town Hall
articles.excerpt: A councillor attended the Full Council meeting at Rochdale Town Hall, advocating for the residents of North Heywood and supporting an amendment to an opposition motion.
articles.content_html:
A councillor attended and spoke at the Full Council meeting at Rochdale Town Hall this week. The councillor represented the residents of North Heywood, advocating for their interests during the session.

During the meeting, the councillor supported an amendment to an opposition motion proposed by the Labour and Co-operative controlling group. This amendment aimed to address specific concerns raised by the community.

The Full Council meeting provided a platform for various issues affecting the ward and the town to be discussed, highlighting the councillor's commitment to local governance.

Further details regarding the outcomes of the meeting or the specific issues addressed were not disclosed in the source material.

articles.title: PVFC Academy to Play Pre-Season Match Against Rochdale
articles.excerpt: PVFC Academy will face Rochdale in their first pre-season game today, marking the final match before heading to London.
articles.content_html:
A football academy will play its first pre-season game today against Rochdale. The match is set to take place away from home and is the final game for the academy before they travel to London for further fixtures.

The academy, known as PVFC Academy, announced the match on social media, expressing excitement for the upcoming game.

This pre-season match is part of the academy's preparations for the new season, allowing players to gain valuable experience against competitive opposition.

Further details about the match, including the time and location, have not been disclosed.

events.title: The Black Dyke Band at Rochdale Town Hall 2027
events.excerpt: In 1871 The Black Dyke Band performed at the original opening ceremony for Rochdale Town Hall. Since the post refurbishment of the Town Hall, the band have since gone on to perform every year bringing the best of brass band quality for all to enjoy. Join them again in 2027.
